title: platform-user — model relationships
description: PlatformUser is the aggregate root for the Stackra-staff plane. Outbound FKs: `identity_id` (reuses the Identity credential substrate), `profile_id` (1:1 PII satellite), `manager_id` (self-referential org chart, nullable). Inbound FKs from `access::PlatformRoleAssignment` (roles on the `platform_admin` guard), `access::PlatformImpersonationSession` (tenant-side act-as sessions, Wave 1b), `audit::Audit` (polymorphic actor pointing at PlatformUsers for platform-plane writes). NEVER carries `tenant_id` or `application_id` — the platform_users table is cross-plane by definition (see tenancy-columns.md §2 exception + design.md §4.3).

internal.description: 1:1 PII satellite. Split from `platform_users` for backup isolation (encrypted-backup workflows target `platform_profiles` separately from the credential-adjacent `platform_users`). Deletion order: PlatformUser first (soft), then PlatformProfile purges only on hard delete of PlatformUser after the 90-day retention hold.
Reverse relation. RESTRICT on delete so we cannot orphan a PlatformUser's PII satellite; the PlatformUser row must be soft-deleted first, then hard-deleted, which cascades PlatformProfile purge.

Self-referential org chart. Nullable — top-of-tree PlatformUsers (heads of department) have no manager. `set null` on delete so offboarding a manager does not cascade-delete their reports; a separate `platform-user:reassign-manager` command handles the reparenting workflow.
Direct reports. Read via the `directReports()` relation on the model.

outbound.description: Reuses the global credential record. `RESTRICT` on delete so a compliance-officer erasing an Identity must first offboard every PlatformUser row referencing that Identity (prevents accidental Identity-side erasure orphaning a staff row). One Identity may back at most ONE PlatformUser (UNIQUE(identity_id) on platform_users) — an Stackra employee is a distinct human, not a projection like User is.

inbound.description: spatie-permission-shaped pivot on the `platform_admin` guard. Cascades on hard delete of the PlatformUser (after the 90-day retention hold) so no orphan role assignments remain. Wave 1b addition.

Real-time act-as sessions where a PlatformUser is impersonating a tenant User. RESTRICT on delete — impersonation sessions are compliance evidence and must outlive the PlatformUser row. On offboarding, the `RevokePlatformUserAccessJob` terminates every ACTIVE impersonation but preserves the historical rows for the retention window. Wave 1b addition.

Polymorphic audit-actor pointer. Every platform-plane write stamps `audit.user_type = App\PlatformUser\Models\PlatformUser` + `audit.user_id = platform_users.id`. Audit rows are RETAINED even after PlatformUser hard delete (see retention.json) — the user_id becomes a dangling reference, resolved via anonymised placeholders.
Same shape as audit but on the shorter-retention activity_log. Every platform-plane write emits an activity_log entry via the `platform.audit` middleware.

accessor_chain.description: PlatformUser exposes computed accessors so downstream code never touches Identity fields directly — preserves the guard boundary + centralises redaction rules.

PlatformUser.reports_count: count of direct reports via hasMany
PlatformUser.on_call_at: resolved via OnCallResolver — timestamp of next on-call shift, null when not in rotation

PlatformProfile.initials: first letter of first_name + first letter of last_name
PlatformProfile.internal_handle: slack_handle when set, else github_handle when set, else first_name
PlatformProfile.phone_e164_masked: phone_e164 with middle digits replaced by • unless caller has `platform.platform_profiles.view.pii`

boundary_note: PlatformUser NEVER references applications, tenants, users, or any tenant-plane row. Any accessor or relation that appears to "scope" a PlatformUser to a tenant is a bug — the platform-admin guard is cross-plane by construction. Impersonation sessions (inbound from `access::PlatformImpersonationSession`) are the ONE legitimate bridge and they still keep the two rows in separate tables.
